  "findings": [
    "Floating floor installed without required expansion gap, causing visible buckling."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove baseboards/trim, cut back the perimeter of the flooring to establish the manufacturer-specified expansion gap (typically 1/4 to 3/8 inch), and reinstall trim."
  ],
  "rationale": "Floating floors require a perimeter expansion gap to accommodate seasonal humidity and temperature changes. Installing the floor tight to the walls prevents natural movement, leading to buckling and irreversible damage to the locking joints.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer Installation Instructions",
    "TCNA Handbook (for applicable underlayment/substrate standards)"
  ]
}
